title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 11 - Trederra Expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Lswdyl9nZtE

Daniel (@dungeondive) covers the Trederra deluxe otherworld expansion — a massive, war-torn alien world that dramatically increases the game's complexity and difficulty. While aesthetically his least favourite expansion ("kind of drab"), he acknowledges it as a fan favourite for players seeking harder combat and more tactical depth.

Trederra introduces cover mechanics, radiation, toxic clouds, landmines, acid rain, and patrol markers that create an escalating ambush threat as heroes push deeper. The world rule adds patrol markers on failed darkness rolls, with die checks determining if enemies spring an ambush. Four enemy types arrive: Trederian Legionnaires (ranged soldiers with faction-specific modifications), Z4 Grenadiers (chunky armoured heavies with thermal charges), Trederian Mutants (dark-stone-corrupted melee berserkers rolling six combat dice), and Lieutenants who issue battlefield orders like air strikes, shock attacks, and traps.

Six warring factions — the Union, the Core, the Carcerist Confederacy, the Royal Foundry, Republic of Taron, and Liberation Army — modify enemy encounters so no two fights feel identical. Daniel compares the setting to Warhammer 40K and Fallout's Brotherhood of Steel. He notes that combining Trederra's cover rules with the Frontier Town expansion's building rules could create a viable skirmish game, and shares his house rule of adding wall-walk and webbing abilities to the otherwise ability-less void spiders.

----

Do you prefer your dungeon crawlers to stay fantasy-pure, or do you enjoy genre-blending like Brimstone's sci-fi otherworlds?
